title: hypericum rigidum subsp. meridionale
intro: Hypericum rigidum subsp. meridionale, also known as the St. John's Wort shrub, is a plant species native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its clusters of small, yellow flowers and woody stems. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antidepressant properties, which are attributed to the presence of bioactive compounds such as hyperforin and hypericin. The medicinal properties of this plant have been linked to several health benefits, including its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of mild to moderate depression. Hypericum rigidum subsp. meridionale can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, tinctures, or topical creams, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
